page: Amazon Web Services m8g.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features a single dedicated physical core and vCPU utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is provisioned at a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the single-core performance of the Neoverse-V2 processor is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show respectable throughput, with bzip3 decompression registering a strong performance. However, because the instance is limited to a single vCPU, multi-core benchmarks, software compilation, and complex operations like ray tracing and image processing rank in the lower performance tiers. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block write speeds and small-block cached reads landing in the poor category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8g.medium offers a highly accessible entry point into the AWS Graviton8 ecosystem, delivering dedicated ARM64 compute resources with low overhead. The lack of bundled local storage and modest network baseline make it a resource-dense option for lightweight workloads where high multi-threaded throughput is not required. This instance is best suited for low-traffic web serving, basic text processing, lightweight database operations, single-threaded utility scripts, and microservices.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.medium is a general-purpose, ARM64-based instance powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 1 dedicated vCPU, 4.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression, though multi-core and memory-intensive operations are limited by the single-vCPU configuration. This instance represents a cost-efficient entry point for lightweight, dedicated ARM64 computing, making it suitable for low-traffic web serving, basic text processing, microservices, and single-threaded utility workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.medium is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 1 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for lightweight workloads.

tagline: A dedicated single-core ARM64 virtual machine offering balanced memory and entry-level compute for lightweight utility workloads and microservices.

4.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded decompression
Best suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and text processing
